title: Online stint-cliff detector features
summary: Recent F1 tyre papers model degradation as latent, compound-specific, and reset by pit stops; change-point literature is designed for abrupt regime shifts.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre papers model degradation as latent, compound-specific, and reset by pit stops; change-point literature is designed for abrupt regime shifts. Pit calls are often triggered by acceleration in pace loss, not just high absolute tyre age.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x Driver` history, build past-only features such as rolling slope and curvature of `LapTime_Delta`, degradation-per-lap acceleration, run-length since last stable regime, and residuals from a simple piecewise or state-space degradation filter that resets after `PitStop==1`.
expected_effect: Adds sensitivity to sudden wear cliffs, evolving track state, and compound-specific breakdowns that current static thresholds miss.
risk: Noisy on very short stints or anomalous laps; must use only past laps to avoid future leakage.
